messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools43
messages.body_plain:
Dear R Windows developers,

there is now a new toolchain for R for Windows, Rtools43. It is now used 
by R-devel and is intended, following likely at least one minor update, 
for R 4.3.0.

Compared to Rtools42, it uses GCC 12 and updates other core components. 
See https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/rtools43/news.html for 
a detailed list of changes.

All users of R-devel who need to compile R packages with source code in 
C, C++ or Fortran should install Rtools43. From the user perspective, 
Rtools43 works the same way as Rtools42. See 
https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/howto-R-devel.html for 
instructions on how to build R-devel and packages for this version of R.

It is recommended to re-install packages that need compilation to avoid 
potential incompatibilities with code built using Rtools42.

Best
Tomas

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools43 (gdal update)
messages.body_plain:
Dear R Windows developers,

a minor update of Rtools43, the toolchain for R for Windows used by 
R-devel, is now available as revision 5493 from

https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/rtools43/rtools.html

The update is important for R-devel users who are building geospatial 
packages from source, because it updates gdal from 3.6.0 to 3.6.2. 
Version 3.6.0 of gdal has been officially retracted, it may cause data 
corruption.

See https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/rtools43/news.html for 
a detailed list of changes.

Best
Tomas
